original_abstract:
This research addresses the growing concern that many students lack a foundational understanding of their religious teachings from primary sources. This knowledge gap often leads to theological disputes and leaves students vulnerable to provocation, frequently resulting in defensive arguments rather than constructive dialogue. To address this, the study examines the Implementation of the Religious Program and Deepening of the Sacred Books (AKPK) at SMPN 1 Darangdan as a means of strengthening students' religious literacy.

Using a descriptive qualitative approach, data was gathered through observations, interviews, and documentation, then analyzed via reduction, data display, and conclusion drawing. The findings reveal that: (1) The program's design effectively aligns with standard management functions—planning, organizing, actuating, and controlling (POAC)—to meet its educational objectives. (2) Key facilitators include the availability of digital and print resources, a dedicated managerial team, and active faculty participation. Conversely, the primary obstacle is the prevalence of Arabic illiteracy among students, which the school addressed by increasing instructional hours. (3) The AKPK program significantly enriches students’ religious values by improving their spiritual relationship with the Divine (*illahiyyah*) and their social conduct with others (*insaniyyah*), while clarifying the central role of worship in their daily lives.
humanized_abstract:
Upaya Peningkatan Kemampuan Beragama Siswa Melalui Implementasi AKPK di SMPN 1 Darangdan The existence of religious disputes and misunderstandings among young generations has become a pressing issue that needs to be addressed. Students who do not understand the religion based on the primary texts are easily exposed to dispute. The disputes often lead to the religious students being cornered and attacked and they have to defend themselves. The defense often turns into an argument and the arguments result in many unnecessary conversations. Therefore, it is necessary to implement the Religious Program and Deepening of the Sacred Books (AKPK) in schools to improve students’ religious literacy.

The research employed a descriptive qualitative approach through the collection, analysis and interpretation of data of an observation of some events, interviews with parties involved and documents. Consequently, the analysis led to reduction, data display and conclusion drawing, and it was found that: The planning, organization, activation and control functions defined in the management activities of the standard POAC were dealt with in a way that supported the desired competences. Facilitators for smooth running of the programme were identified as, the on line and printed materials, the managerial committee of the school and the active participation of faculty members. Inhibiting factors were also identified and one of them was Arabic illiteracy, which was later resolved by the extending school hours. The research found out that the AKPK programme contributed to developing the Islamic values among its students by strengthening the relationship with the Divine (illahiyyah) and improving the relations between humans (insaniyyah) in addition to clarifying the position of worship in lives of its students.
